title: SQL Repositories, Pools, Transactions, and Request Consistency · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer sql repositories, pools, transactions, and request consistency into a new context-bound repository and transaction contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of context-bound repository and transaction contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Treat sql.DB as a concurrent pool, open once, verify readiness, tune open and idle capacity and lifetime, observe Stats, and close at process shutdown.
Use dialect parameters, QueryContext, Scan, rows Close and Err, bounded result sets, and error identity without constructing SQL from input.
Define atomic invariants, BeginTx with context and isolation, defer rollback, commit exactly once, and handle PostgreSQL Read Committed changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Db Handle Pool Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 7599 in a recovery rehearsal with an ambiguous database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify transaction outcome without retrying an ambiguous commit; then cancel during the dependency phase and preserve cleanup plus response ownership. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.

hints: For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Request Commit Ambiguity, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Request Commit Ambiguity precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Request Commit Ambiguity, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A lab team handles case 7608 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify transaction outcome without retrying an ambiguous commit; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Handle canceled requests and ambiguous commit results with idempotency, reconciliation, durable identifiers, and no claim unsupported by storage evidence.

For Go server Defend the transferred model · Lab Request Commit Ambiguity, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: If the client disconnects before the response, the database transaction necessarily rolled back.
